LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE RECYCLING & DISPOSAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES MOTION, MOTION IS GRANTED, AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL STIPULATES TO RULING. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED, THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THERE IS ANYTHING BEYOND CCP SECTION 437C(F)I. TRIABLE ISSUES EXISTS ON WHETHER DEFENDANT SAW PLAINTIFF AS DISABLED; WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS QUALIFIED WORKER; ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER LEAVE WAS ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION; AND, WHETHER DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN INTERACTIVE PROCESS WITH PLAINTIFF? PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE RECYCLING & DISPOSAL COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Edward J. Nevin, Esq. and Kenneth C. Absalom, Esq., of the Law Offices of Nevin & Absalom, (415) 392-5040, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; William J. Dritsas, of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, (415) 544-1027, appearing for Defendant, Golden Gate Recycling & Disposal Company, and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is denied. As to punitive damages motion, motion is granted, and plaintiff's counsel stipulates to ruling. The Motion for summary adjudication is denied, there is no showing that there is anything beyond CCP section 437c(f)i. Triable issues exists on whether Defendant saw Plaintiff as disabled; Whether Plaintiff is qualified worker; issues regarding whether Leave was Adverse Employment Action; and, whether Defendant engaged in interactive process with plaintiff? Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
